EN.title: Vault TV
EN.description: Streaming-only VaultWares thin client for Fire TV, Android TV, and remote-control-first devices.
EN.body:
# Vault TV

Last updated: Wed, 01 Jul 2026 00:10 America/Toronto.

Vault TV is the standalone TV client for the VaultWares media stack. It is a
thin client: it renders catalog, details, search, playback, and settings UI for
remote-control-first devices while server-side VaultWares services keep provider
lookup, torrent/debrid resolution, stream signing, and secrets out of the device
bundle.

## Project record

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Repository | `p-potvin/vault-tv` |
| Local checkout | `C:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\Github Repos\vault-tv` |
| Jira project key | `VTV` |
| App class | TV web client; Android TV / Fire TV package target |
| Theme source | `vaultwares-themes` submodule |
| Current status | Web-first milestone implemented; Jira project creation blocked until Jira credentials/tooling are available in the agent session |

## Boundaries

Vault TV must call only VaultWares-controlled media APIs and playback URLs
returned by those APIs.

The client must not call Real-Debrid, AllDebrid, Premiumize, Torrentio, Jackett,
Prowlarr, Bitmagnet, MediaFusion, YTS, EZTV, provider mirrors, or direct
torrent/debrid endpoints. Those lookups go through Comet and the VaultWares
media stack.

## Current milestone

The first milestone is a web-first TV shell:

- React, Vite, and TypeScript app scaffold
- D-pad-friendly media grid navigation
- mock catalog/details/search/player/settings flows
- guarded media client that rejects non-VaultWares API bases
- pairing token helper boundary
- `vaultwares-themes` submodule for canonical design tokens
- tests covering focus navigation, media-client guardrails, and player state

## Jira and sync

The intended Jira project is:

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Jira key | `VTV` |
| Jira name | `Vault TV` |
| Jira type | Team-managed Kanban |
| GitHub repo | `p-potvin/vault-tv` |

After the Jira project exists, update `vw-jira-sync` and run:

```bash
python scripts/create_projects.py --repo vault-tv
python scripts/backfill.py --repo vault-tv
```

Add the repository webhook to `https://hooks.vaultwares.ca/github` with the
standard VaultWares GitHub webhook secret from VaultWarden. Do not paste the
secret into docs, commits, Jira, or ledger entries.

## Related docs

- `/operations/jira-sync`
- `/operations/services-inventory`
- `/ai-tools/assistant-protocols/project-creation`
- `/ai-tools/assistant-protocols/submodule-boundaries`
- `/ai-tools/assistant-protocols/brand-tokens-ui`

QC.title: Vault TV
QC.description: Client mince de streaming VaultWares pour Fire TV, Android TV, et appareils controles a la telecommande.
QC.body:
# Vault TV

Derniere mise a jour : Wed, 01 Jul 2026 00:10 America/Toronto.

Vault TV est le client TV autonome pour la pile media VaultWares. C'est un
client mince : il affiche le catalogue, les details, la recherche, la lecture,
et les reglages pour les appareils controles a la telecommande, pendant que les
services VaultWares cote serveur gardent la recherche de fournisseurs, la
resolution torrent/debrid, la signature des flux, et les secrets hors du bundle
de l'appareil.

## Fiche projet

| Champ | Valeur |
|---|---|
| Depot | `p-potvin/vault-tv` |
| Checkout local | `C:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\Github Repos\vault-tv` |
| Cle Jira | `VTV` |
| Classe d'app | Client web TV; cible Android TV / Fire TV |
| Source de theme | Sous-module `vaultwares-themes` |
| Etat actuel | Premier jalon web implemente; creation du projet Jira bloquee tant que les identifiants/outils Jira ne sont pas disponibles dans la session agent |

## Limites

Vault TV doit appeler seulement les API media controlees par VaultWares et les
URL de lecture retournees par ces API.

Le client ne doit pas appeler Real-Debrid, AllDebrid, Premiumize, Torrentio,
Jackett, Prowlarr, Bitmagnet, MediaFusion, YTS, EZTV, des miroirs de
fournisseurs, ou des endpoints torrent/debrid directs. Ces recherches passent
par Comet et la pile media VaultWares.

## Jalon courant

Le premier jalon est une coquille web pour TV :

- app React, Vite, et TypeScript
- navigation de grille media compatible D-pad
- flux simules de catalogue, details, recherche, lecture, et reglages
- client media garde qui refuse les bases API hors VaultWares
- limite pour les jetons de jumelage
- sous-module `vaultwares-themes` pour les jetons de design canoniques
- tests pour la navigation de focus, les garde-fous du client media, et l'etat du lecteur

## Jira et synchronisation

Le projet Jira prevu est :

| Champ | Valeur |
|---|---|
| Cle Jira | `VTV` |
| Nom Jira | `Vault TV` |
| Type Jira | Kanban gere par equipe |
| Depot GitHub | `p-potvin/vault-tv` |

Apres la creation du projet Jira, mettez a jour `vw-jira-sync` et lancez :

```bash
python scripts/create_projects.py --repo vault-tv
python scripts/backfill.py --repo vault-tv
```

Ajoutez le webhook du depot vers `https://hooks.vaultwares.ca/github` avec le
secret webhook GitHub standard de VaultWarden. Ne collez pas le secret dans les
docs, commits, Jira, ou entrees ledger.

## Docs liees

- `/operations/jira-sync`
- `/operations/services-inventory`
- `/ai-tools/assistant-protocols/project-creation`
- `/ai-tools/assistant-protocols/submodule-boundaries`
- `/ai-tools/assistant-protocols/brand-tokens-ui`
